danger_signals.visual_form: Visual manifestation of silver armlet artifact in game world and held by antagonist
danger_signals.meaning: Indicates active threat and chaos/destruction magic in use; marks locations of danger and evil influence

danger_signals.visual_form: Hostile entities appearing in traversable map areas
danger_signals.meaning: Direct combat threats requiring player engagement or avoidance

danger_signals.visual_form: Special weapons (swords, crossbows, bombs) breaking after set usage
danger_signals.meaning: Resource scarcity signals danger ahead; limited magical armament indicates vulnerability periods

safety_signals.visual_form: Gold armlet artifact held by protagonist Prince Ali
safety_signals.meaning: Player empowerment signal; represents legitimacy of quest and capacity to oppose silver armlet evil

safety_signals.visual_form: Environmental markers where four spirits (Dytto, Efreet, Shade, Bow) are found
safety_signals.meaning: Safe zones offering progression; acquiring spirits indicates power-up and quest advancement
Resource availability signals safe progression; restoration capability eases traversal

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Shrines dedicated to four elemental spirits; areas under spiritual protection
Areas of chaos and destruction; locations marked by evil magic influence; enemy creature concentrations

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Buried gold armlet discovery; overgrown or abandoned ancient wizard locations
Increased enemy presence; areas of visible destruction and chaos in environment
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Escalating hostile encounters; urgency in NPC dialogue and quest direction

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Shrine locations where spirits manifest; visual feedback of each spirit type (water, fire, shadow, plant)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Increased combat capability; access to new areas previously blocked; summoning animations for each spirit

environmental_storytelling.example: Gold armlet vs. silver armlet visual distinction communicates moral polarity and ancient wizard legacy without exposition
Distribution of four spirit shrines (water, fire, shadow, plant) across map tells story of balanced magic system and wizard's ancient protections
Special weapons breaking after use creates narrative through gameplay of resource scarcity and world danger level
Gradual unlocking of new areas as spirits are acquired visually demonstrates world state change from threat-controlled to player-liberated territories

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Default state; limited attack capability; vulnerable positioning
Enhanced combat animations; fire-ignition capability with certain weapons; increased aggression options
Access to elemental magic effects; visual aura or animation indicating active spirit power
Aggressive movement toward player; attack animations; patrol patterns in danger zones
Increased aggression and density in specific map areas; behavioral clustering around armlet wielder locations

notes: Beyond Oasis uses a binary faction system (gold vs. silver armlet) to communicate moral and magical conflict. The game employs elemental spirit acquisition as primary progression signal, with each spirit unlock representing both power increase and narrative advancement. Environmental design distinguishes safe shrine spaces from hostile world zones. Weapon degradation creates vulnerability pacing that reinforces world danger state. The game's visual communication relies heavily on artifact symbolism (gold/silver polarity) and elemental visual effects rather than detailed faction imagery or complex world-state indicators—typical of early 1990s action-adventure design.
